mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth reflects labor income expansion that drives consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations; a rising PAYEMS level causes positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity discount rates remain stable.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes fail to show positive rank correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent signals a zero lower bound environment where unconventional monetary policy suppresses risk-free rates and boosts equity risk appetite, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0% or t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales reveal a coordinated supply-demand contraction that forces negative earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When INDPRO and RSAFS jointly decline, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of signal instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 6.0 percent, an increase in the unemployment rate amplifies consumption downside risk and fiscal stress, causing negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns through earnings multiple compression.
failure_condition: During UNRATE > 6.0% regimes, rising UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of months, or the signal Sharpe ratio is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index reduces real corporate earnings and raises the probability of monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days via discount rate expansion.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes exhibit a positive rank correlation with 5-day SPX forward returns (Rank IC > 0) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 850 thousand units signal durable construction sector momentum and household formation that drives cyclical earnings growth, causing positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns through revenue surprise channels.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 850, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production concurrent with rising unemployment identifies a recessionary supply-labor contraction that triggers broad risk-off repositioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO concurrent with increases in UNRATE fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns (hit rate < 55% or Rank IC > 0) over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in real GDP captures aggregate demand shocks that drive upward revisions to corporate cash flows; a rising GDPC1 level causes positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days via earnings expansion.
failure_condition: GDPC1 changes show negative rank correlation with 60-day SPX forward returns (Rank IC < -0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.
